text: {"suggestedLabel":"Empirical Skepticism","suggestedDescription":"A cautious skeptic who trusts only direct empirical observation and doubts theoretical extrapolations","stances":[{"claimIndex":0,"stance":"reject","rationale":"Without direct observation of black holes from cosmic rays, the claim remains unverified."},{"claimIndex":1,"stance":"reject","rationale":"A modified metric is a theoretical construct lacking empirical support."},{"claimIndex":2,"stance":"accept","rationale":"The lack of established theory predicting black‑hole production aligns with the view that it requires speculative physics."},{"claimIndex":3,"stance":"reject","rationale":"Large extra dimensions are a speculative hypothesis not confirmed by observation."},{"claimIndex":4,"stance":"accept","rationale":"No experiment has directly detected Hawking radiation, so the statement matches observed facts."},{"claimIndex":5,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"We have no empirical evidence of microscopic black holes to confirm their evaporation behavior."},{"claimIndex":6,"stance":"uncertain","rationale":"The dynamics of hypothetical black holes from cosmic rays versus the LHC are not directly observed."},{"claimIndex":7,"stance":"accept","rationale":"The analysis indeed includes both charged and neutral black holes as described."},{"claimIndex":8,"stance":"accept","rationale":"Cosmic‑ray energies exceeding LHC collisions are well‑documented by observations."},{"claimIndex":9,"stance":"accept","rationale":"The long‑term survival of white dwarfs and neutron stars provides empirical limits on harmful black‑hole accretion."}]}
